Exchange an authorization code for a Verdex access token (login/signup flow).

The frontend is responsible for generating and validating the OAuth ``state``
parameter (CSRF protection).  This endpoint only handles the code exchange
and user creation/lookup.
Exchange an authorization code to link a provider account to the current user.

get.description:
Return all of the current user's connections to the given provider.

Tokens are never included in the response.

delete.description: Disconnect a specific provider account from the current user's account.

get.description: Return the GitHub App installation URL.

Return all repositories connected by the current user for a given provider.

Each entry includes the latest grade and score for quick display.
Returns an empty list (not 404) if no repositories have been connected yet.

post.description:
Connect a repository to Verdex.

For GitHub supply github_repo_id (integer).
For GitLab/Bitbucket supply provider_repo_id (string) and set provider accordingly.

Idempotent — returns the existing record if the repo is already connected.
Installs a webhook on the provider (unique secret per repo, stored encrypted).

parameters.name: github_repo_id

schema.description: GitHub numeric repository ID (use for provider=github)
schema.title: Github Repo Id

parameters.description: GitHub numeric repository ID (use for provider=github)

parameters.name: provider_repo_id

schema.description: Provider repository ID as string (use for gitlab/bitbucket)
schema.title: Provider Repo Id

parameters.description: Provider repository ID as string (use for gitlab/bitbucket)

get.description:
Proxy to the provider's repository listing API.

Returns repositories the user has access to but may not have connected
to Verdex yet.  Used by the frontend to populate the "Add Repository" dialog.

delete.description:
Disconnect a repository from Verdex.

Removes the provider webhook (best-effort) and deletes the Repository
record, which cascades to all WebhookEvents.

patch.description: Update mutable repository settings (e.g. webhook_active toggle).

get.description: Return the per-repository settings for a connected repository.

patch.description: Update the per-repository settings (pass grade, PR comments, status checks).

post.description:
Receive and queue a GitHub webhook delivery.

Returns 202 immediately after persisting the event.
Returns 200 with {"status": "duplicate"} if this delivery ID was already processed.
Returns 401 if the HMAC signature is invalid.
Returns 404 if the repository is not registered with Verdex.

Receive and queue a GitLab webhook delivery.

Authentication: plaintext X-Gitlab-Token header (per-project secret).
Returns 401 if the token does not match the stored secret.
Returns 404 if the repository is not registered with Verdex.

Receive and queue a Bitbucket Cloud webhook delivery.

Authentication: HMAC-SHA256 via X-Hub-Signature (same algorithm as GitHub).
Returns 401 if the signature does not match.
Returns 404 if the repository is not registered with Verdex.

get.description:
Return aggregate stats for the current user's dashboard.

Scoped to the authenticated user's connected repositories.
Returns an empty dashboard if the user has no GitHub connection.

post.description:
Queue an on-demand analysis for a repository.

Returns immediately with the Celery task ID and a pre-created report ID.
Poll GET /tasks/{task_id} to track progress.

get.description: Return paginated analysis reports for a repository, newest first.
Return a single analysis report.
Return all violations for an analysis report.

Return the Celery task status for a queued analysis.

Possible states: PENDING, STARTED, SUCCESS, FAILURE, RETRY.

Return Rego rules from the DB registry.

Optionally filter to only enabled rules.
Return Verdex-created pull requests for a repository, newest first.

Return per-repository rule overrides (explicit enable/disable settings).

Rules not listed here inherit the global enabled state from the rules registry.

patch.description:
Set per-repository rule enable/disable overrides.

Accepts a list of {rule_id, enabled} objects. Each entry is upserted —
existing overrides are updated, new ones are created.

To reset a rule to its global default, remove it via the list (omit it
from a subsequent PATCH — there is no explicit delete endpoint for now).

get.description: List all cloud provider connections for the current user.

post.description: Register a new cloud provider connection with encrypted credentials.

delete.description: Delete a cloud connection and all its associated reports.

post.description: Queue a static IaC analysis for a repository's Terraform files.
Queue a dynamic live-resource analysis for a cloud connection.

get.description: List all cloud analysis reports for the current user.
Get a single cloud analysis report.
List violations for a cloud analysis report.
List all cloud Rego rules, optionally filtered by platform.
Return the latest energy score for a repository.
Return the daily score history for a repository (newest first).

Return the detailed energy breakdown for a repository's latest analysis.

Includes per-dimension scores with human-readable labels, raw energy/carbon
figures, and the grid intensity used in the calculation.

Returns 404 if no analysis has been run yet.

post.description:
Generate (or return cached) an AI fix suggestion for a violation.

On first call: fetches the workflow YAML from GitHub, calls the LLM,
caches and returns the result.
On subsequent calls: returns the cached result immediately.
Set force=true to regenerate (e.g. after switching LLM providers).

Returns 503 if the configured LLM provider has no API key set.
Returns 502 if the LLM API call fails.

parameters.name: force

schema.description: Set to true to regenerate even if a cached suggestion exists.
schema.title: Force

parameters.description: Set to true to regenerate even if a cached suggestion exists.

get.description:
Return the cached AI fix suggestion for a violation (no LLM call).

Returns 404 if no suggestion has been generated yet.
Use POST /violations/{id}/fix to generate one.

post.description:
Generate AI fix suggestions for all unresolved violations in a report.

Triggers fix generation in-process for each violation that doesn't already
have a cached suggestion.  Returns a summary of how many fixes were
generated vs. already cached.

This is a synchronous endpoint that may take up to ~30 s for large reports.
For very large reports, consider generating fixes per-violation instead.

Create a GitHub PR with all AI-generated fixes for a completed report.

Workflow:
  1. Load all AI fix suggestions for the report's violations
  2. For each violation with a suggestion, apply the fix to the workflow YAML
     (search-and-replace on the fix_hint / original section)
  3. Create a branch verdex/fix-{report_short_id}
  4. Commit modified workflow files
  5. Open (or update) a PR — returns the VerdexPullRequest record

Requires auto_pr_enabled=True on the repository (default).
Returns 422 if no AI fixes have been generated yet.
Returns 422 if auto_pr_enabled is False.

get.description:
Return the public energy profile for a repository.

Requires the repository to be connected to Verdex and have been analysed
at least once. Returns 404 if the repository is not registered or has no
score yet.

This endpoint is public — no authentication required.

Return the top-scoring public repositories on Verdex.

Sorted by overall_score descending (best first), then by calculated_at
descending as a tiebreaker. Only public repositories are included.

This endpoint is public — no authentication required.

post.description:
Immediately trigger a rescan of all webhook-active repositories.

Dispatches the same Celery task that Celery Beat runs every 6 hours,
with staggered countdowns to avoid thundering-herd pressure.

Requires superuser.

get.description:
Return system-wide statistics.

Requires superuser.

post.description:
Create a new telemetry API key for a repository.

Returns the plaintext key exactly once — it is not stored and cannot be
retrieved later.  Store it in a CI secret immediately.

get.description: Return all active API keys for a repository (hashes are never shown).

post.description:
Ingest a telemetry data point from a CI runner.

Authenticated via the X-Verdex-Api-Key header.  Create keys via
POST /telemetry/repositories/{repo_id}/api-keys.

Rate-limited to 500 requests per minute per repository.
Returns 401 if the key is missing, unknown, or revoked.

get.description: Return recent telemetry data points for a repository.
Return all organizations the current user belongs to.

post.description: Create an organization. The caller becomes its owner.

get.description: Return an organization (must be a member).
List all members of an organization.

post.description: Add a user to the organization (owner/admin only).

delete.description: Remove a user from the organization (owner/admin, or self-leave).

get.description: Return paginated audit log entries for the organization (owner/admin only).

Opt a repository out of Verdex cold outreach PRs.

No authentication required — anyone can opt out a repo by full name.
The repo is added to a persistent blocklist; any open cold PRs from
verdex-bot will be closed with a polite comment within 24 hours.

Idempotent: calling this multiple times for the same repo is safe.

parameters.name: repo

schema.description: Repository to opt out in 'owner/repo' format.
schema.title: Repo

parameters.description: Repository to opt out in 'owner/repo' format.

post.description:
Submit a free AI fix request to the OSS maintainer queue.

Eligibility requirements (enforced at processing time):
- Repository must be public
- Minimum activity signal: ≥50 GitHub stars, or 3+ contributors,
  or affiliation with Apache / CNCF / Linux Foundation

Only one active request per user is allowed at a time.  If you already
have a queued or processing request, this endpoint returns 409.

The fix is generated during off-peak hours; you will receive an email
when it is ready with a one-click "open PR" button.

get.description:
Return the status and queue position of an OSS fix request.

Queue position is 1-indexed and recomputed on every call.
Once status becomes 'completed', pr_url will contain the generated PR link.
